name: Psychological Thriller
description: Unreliable narrator, memory gaps, gaslighting, twists that reframe everything

entities.name: The Protagonist - Unreliable
entities.description: Seems normal but has gaps. Blackouts. Missing time. May be perpetrator or victim. Or both.

attributes.condition: dissociative episodes / medication side effects / trauma

entities.name: The Partner / Gaslighter
entities.description: Charming, supportive, concerned. But details don't add up. Protective or controlling?

entities.name: The Anchor - Outside Witness
entities.description: Old friend / therapist / detective / neighbor. Only one who believes protagonist. Targeted.

entities.name: The Family Home
entities.description: Beautiful, isolated, smart-home everything. Cameras everywhere. Protagonist's prison/sanctuary.

entities.name: The Therapy Office / Safe Space
entities.description: Only place without cameras. Where protagonist speaks freely. May be compromised.

plot_threads.title: The Missing Time
plot_threads.description: Protagonist loses hours/days. Finds evidence of actions they don't remember.

plot_threads.title: The Medication Mystery
plot_threads.description: Pills changed. Side effects include memory loss. Who prescribes? Who benefits?

plot_threads.title: The Previous Victim
plot_threads.description: Protagonist discovers partner's ex disappeared/died. Similar circumstances.

plot_threads.title: The Truth About the 'Accident'
plot_threads.description: Traumatic event that started it all wasn't an accident. Protagonist caused it - or was framed.

world_rules.rule: Memory is reconstructive, not reproductive. Every recall changes the memory.
world_rules.exceptions: Trauma memories can be fragmented but core emotional truth remains

world_rules.rule: Smart home records everything. Cloud backups exist even if local deleted.
world_rules.exceptions: Faraday cage / powered off / analog only

world_rules.rule: Gaslighter makes victim question reality. Small lies -> big lies. Isolation is key.
Incompetency declarations can strip autonomy. Partner can petition.

foreshadowing.content: Protospet notices partner's reflection in window - smiling when they think she's not looking
Prescription bottle label smudged. Dosage changed but doctor denies it.
Anchor character mentions 'your partner called me, said you're having episode again' - protagonist has no memory
